name: Run as Domain User
description: A wrapper app that uses ShellRunAs to launch a target executable under a defined Active Directory domain user from non-domain-joined Windows devices. It enables cloud-managed devices to run legacy on-prem tools (RSAT) by prompting for domain credentials and launching the configured app with elevated rights. It relies on simple domain.txt and app.txt configuration and is packaged for Intune with explicit install/uninstall commands and a Start Menu entry.
keywords: how to run rsat on azure ad joined device
troubleshoot domain credentials on cloud device
active directory tools on entra joined pc
install rsat on intune managed device
author: Niklas Rast

securityCheck.aiSummary: Two notable security concerns in the Run as Domain User flow: (1) invocation of a PowerShell script with -ExecutionPolicy Bypass, which can weaken defenses if not strictly controlled; (2) unvalidated external inputs used to build command-line arguments for a domain-privileged helper process, which could be exploited to run unintended code. No evidence of remote script download, credential harvesting, data exfiltration, or hardcoded secrets is present. The tooling aligns with Intune/ManagementExtension patterns, but the bypass and input handling should be tightened (code signing, allow-lists, least-privilege execution) to reduce risk in a managed environment.
